core_principles: Filter models based on specific criteria such as model architecture, dataset used for training, and performance metrics
Use a range of evaluation metrics to assess the performance of generative models
Use models that can generate outputs in a format that can be easily consumed by downstream agents or applications
Periodically update models with new data using model rotation
Fine-tune pre-trained models for specific tasks and datasets
operational_workflow: Discover open generative models using the Hugging Face Model Hub or paperswithcode.com/sota
Filter models based on specific criteria and evaluate them using relevant metrics
Select the best-performing model and fine-tune it for the specific task or dataset
Deploy the model and use it for inference, generating outputs in a format that can be easily consumed by downstream agents or applications
Periodically update the model with new data using model rotation to maintain its performance and adapt to changing conditions

tool_routing.avoid: Models that are not open-source or have limited evaluation metrics

failure_modes.cause: Lack of model updates or rotation
failure_modes.prevention: Regularly update models with new data using model rotation

prompt_templates.standard: Generate an image/text/audio clip based on the input prompt
prompt_templates.high_quality: Generate a high-quality image/text/audio clip based on the input prompt, using a fine-tuned model
prompt_templates.fast: Generate an image/text/audio clip based on the input prompt, using a fast and efficient model

benchmark_criteria: Inception Score (IS) and Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) for image generation
BLEU score and ROUGE score for text generation
update_triggers: Changes in the task or dataset requirements
learning_emphasis: How to discover evaluate and rotate open generative models for image text and TTS with agent-friendly inference
